Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Cornell University Library records, [ca.1868]-2010.
Collection Number: 13-1-1082

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell University Library records, [ca.1868]-2010.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
13-1-1082
Abstract:
Correspondence relating to the development and administration of the library, general administrative files, administrative files of Librarians, financial records, statistical reports, and grant files.
Creator:
Cornell University. Library. Administration
Kaser, David, 1924-2017.
Kinkeldey, Otto, 1878-1966.
Martin, Louis E.
McCarthy, Stephen A. (Stephen Anthony), 1908-1990.
Miller, J. Gormly.
Seznec, Alain.
Eden, Barbara Berger
Quanitities:
117.3 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Correspondence relating to the development and administration of the library, general administrative files, administrative files of Librarians Otto Kinkeldey and Stephen McCarthy, financial records, statistical reports, and grant files. Also, records pertaining to the construction of the John M. Olin Library, including correspondence and reports of the Ad Hoc Committee on the Library Building Program; preliminary drawings; architectural drawings and blueprints; booklets, brochures, and papers relating to the dedication ceremonies; Library of Congress files, 1958-1986; ALA and ARL files, 1949-1985; Collection Development and Management Project files and user survey, 1978-1981; file relating to Cornell's decision to join RLG, 1978-1979; scrapbook of clippings of events connected with the library, 1984-1990; results of a poll of library employees, ca. 1975; files of the Librarian (primarily Louis Martin and Gormly Miller) relating to departments in Olin Library including Circulation, History of Science, Icelandic, Interlibrary Loan, Manuscripts and University Archives, Maps, Microtexts, and Newspapers, the New York Historical Resources Center, and Reference, 1968-1989.
RLG files include materials relating to BibTech, Conspectus, Shared Cataloging Program, Preservation Committee, and Cultural Materials Alliance, ca. 1984-2006
Also recognition by the Council for Advancement and Support of Education (CASE) for superior accomplishments, 1983; and Libraries at Cornell brochure, 1984.
Also, photo album of a 1990 visit by Asian dignitaries, a 1980 User Survey, a code book of detailed work done by several departments (1891-1923) including special collection bookplates, and slides and audiocassettes describing the library.
Also Excellence in Academic Libraries Award certificate from ACRL (Association of College & Research Libraries) and library brochure.
Memorabilia includes pen used to sign an cooperation agreement with Tsinghua University in China, 2009 and various ribbons, buttons, etc.
Files of Karen Calhoun on the Library's Models for Academic Support (MAS 2010) project, 2002-2004; Library Management Team, 2001-2006; and Technical Services Executive Group files, 1999-2006.
Conservation and Preservation Department Grant files of Barbara Eden.
RELATED MATERIALS

See also Cornell University Library Records, #13-1-17. Seel also Cornell University Olin, Kroch, Uris Libraries administrative records, #13-1-3356.
NOTES

Includes collection #13-1-1287.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell University Library Records, #13-1-1082. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:

Access restricted to the permission of office of origin with the exception of Boxes 58-70, 73.

Research Libraries Group
Carl A. Kroch Library (Cornell University)
Subjects:
Academic librarians -- New York (State) -- Ithaca
Library finance.
Library buildings.
Library architecture.
Library administration.
Academic libraries -- Statistics.
Academic libraries -- Administration.

Series II. Grant Files
II.a. Grant Files
Scope and Contents
These files are ordered chronologically by the dates of the official grant period. The files within each category therefore have dates both before and after the date of the grant, as preliminary and followup information is included in these files. In some cases where there are several files for one grant, the files are not arranged chronologically, but are instead grouped by complementary materials.
Scope and Contents
Grant titles appear in bold, with corresponding folders below them.

CPA

Box 128
Lynn, M. Stuart and Kenney, A. Making of America
July 2, 1993-December 31, 1996
Scope and Contents
Charles E. Culpeper Foundation L89-8511. This project focused on material from 1860 - 1960.
Box 128 Folder 19
Culpeper
 1992 - August 10, 1995
Scope and Contents
Correspondence with the Culpeper foundation; a 1992 report about Phase I of the Cornell/Xerox Commission project; a summer 1992 copy of CIT News about electronic publishing; a Cornell booklet "Creating the Future" from 1991 outlining Cornell's goal for 75 million dollars in donations. Copies of this material were presumably sent to accompany the grant request for the Making of America project.
Box 128 Folder 20
Making of America Folder 1
 April 1993 - June 1994
Scope and Contents
Executive summary, a copy of the proposal, and financial reports.
Box 128 Folder 21
Making of America Folder 2
 April 1993 - May 12, 1997
Box 128 Folder 22
Culpeper Correspondence
 July 1, 1993 - July 1995

Series III. Library Directors/University Librarians Correspondence
Scope and Contents
Boxes 110-111: Please note that these files have been edited. Correspondence relating staff disciplinary issues and salary have been removed. Also removed was correspondence which had little subject matter other than a vague thank you or reference to previous correspondence. These files result from central administrative file "purging" in October 1999. Other correspondence can be found in administrative files that were sent to Archives prior to that time.
